entries.title: Initial repository baseline
entries.summary: Repository contains a validated 21-Standard-Skill/12-workflow system, 27 reusable Custom Skills, scoped activation, onboarding, governance, and validation; no application source exists.
evidence: Recursive inventory before initialization returned COUNT=0; file discovery returned no paths; git status reported no repository.
The UI/UX audit verified the installed skill/routing and continued absence of application UI sources.
The backend audit verified the installed backend skill/routing and continued absence of application/Base44 sources.
Git repository and reusable governance artifacts now exist; unified local validation covers 21 skills, 12 workflows, 24 routing fixtures, 35 anti-patterns, repository hygiene, links, and memory.
Custom validation covers 27 reusable modules, 14 technology detections, 13 domain detections, 12 custom route cases, and one HIGH-confidence GitHub CI binding.
entries.rationale: Prevents later agents from inventing an application architecture from an instruction-only workspace.
consequences: Use project-onboarding immediately after cloning; re-run application discovery and specialist audits when real sources arrive.
risks: This baseline becomes stale when a clone is not instantiated or application evidence is added without updating the profile.

entries.title: Evidence-backed project memory is mandatory
entries.summary: Every task retrieves and verifies scoped memory, reports conflicts, writes material deltas, and stores no secrets or private reasoning.
evidence: Repository instructions and bootstrap explicitly gate work on memory retrieval/verification and prohibit secrets and chain-of-thought.
entries.rationale: Continuity must survive chat boundaries without weakening authoritative sources or safety.
consequences: Medium/complex implementation is blocked until the Memory Context Summary is complete.
risks: Skipping routing can reintroduce stale assumptions.

entries.title: Application and specialist instruction baseline is missing
entries.summary: Application code, business/Base44/schema/auth/test/deployment sources remain unavailable; the UI and backend procedural skill gaps are closed.
evidence: The initial empty-workspace audit found none of the expected application sources.
UI/UX Production now exists and is routed; application-specific UI and the remaining backend/business/schema/test/deployment sources are still absent.
Backend Production Engineering now exists and is routed; application-specific backend/Base44 sources remain absent.
entries.rationale: The missing-source boundary must remain explicit so agents do not fabricate project knowledge.
consequences: Specialist procedures are available, but application semantics and production readiness remain unverified until source onboarding.
risks: Implementation without source ingestion may violate unknown requirements.

entries.title: Application context is unknown
entries.summary: Product, application stack/modules/contracts/auth/Base44 capabilities/deployment/tests and application UI/backend architecture remain unknown; both production procedures now exist.
evidence: No application or approved project sources were present during audit; the UI procedural skill does not establish application-specific design facts.
No application or approved Base44/backend sources were present during audit; the backend procedural skill does not establish application-specific capabilities or facts.
entries.rationale: Explicit unknowns are safer than inferred business or technical facts.
consequences: Source onboarding is required before project-specific implementation decisions.
risks: The question list may need splitting by domain after sources arrive.

entries.title: Project semantic and episodic memory is unpopulated
entries.summary: Project semantic/episodic and application-specific UI/backend memory remain unpopulated despite Project Memory and both procedural specialist skills being available.
evidence: The general and UI gap audits list missing application sources and source-backed closure criteria.
The backend audit/risk/backlog record missing application/Base44/domain/schema/auth/runtime sources and source-backed closure criteria.
entries.rationale: Recording the debt makes the next onboarding task actionable and prevents false completion.
consequences: First source-bearing task must pay the onboarding/verification cost.
risks: If ignored, the store remains procedural but not project-semantic.

entries.title: Use deterministic JSON memory index
entries.summary: Use Markdown detail plus a deterministic JSON index; do not add vector infrastructure without measured need and approval.
evidence: The approved task constraints require deterministic indexing first and prohibit unapproved vector database or major dependency additions.
entries.rationale: The current small local store needs deterministic, dependency-free retrieval and validation.
consequences: Detail and index updates are atomic; future search must preserve deterministic fallback.
risks: Metadata quality determines retrieval quality.

entries.title: Progressive memory retrieval
entries.summary: Retrieve index metadata, then summary, then detail, then verify authoritative sources.
evidence: The skill, retrieval policy, and two-layer index implement the same progressive route.
entries.rationale: Selective expansion reduces context while preserving source verification.
consequences: Agents should not load unrelated memory details.
risks: Poor triggers or stale index metadata can hide relevant entries.

entries.title: Bootstrap Project Memory operating system
entries.summary: Created and validated the project-local memory routing, policies, contracts, templates, deterministic index, baseline audits, and lint workflow.
evidence: Memory lint passed with 0 blockers and 0 warnings; an invalid fixture missing trigger_terms was rejected; skill-creator quick validation logic reported Skill is valid.
entries.rationale: A compact completed summary preserves the implementation and verification boundary without retaining conversational working state.
consequences: Future tasks are routed through evidence-based memory before specialist work.
risks: Application-specific memory remains unavailable until code and approved project sources arrive.

entries.title: Onboard application sources and specialist instructions
entries.summary: Blocked until authoritative application, Base44, business, schema/auth, test, runtime, and deployment sources are available; both specialist production skills are installed and routed.
evidence: The initial recursive inventory was empty.
UI/UX Production was created and routed; application and Backend sources remain absent.
Backend Production Engineering was created and routed; repository discovery still found no application/Base44 sources.
entries.rationale: The task specification requires source-backed codebase and FE/BE audits; those steps cannot be completed by fabrication.
consequences: Resume application onboarding immediately when authoritative sources are added.
risks: Implementing application behavior before onboarding may violate unknown business, security, data, platform, or production rules.

entries.title: UI/UX Production installed; application UI baseline absent
entries.summary: UI/UX Production is mandatory and routed; no application framework, tokens, components, product flows, or UI tests exist to tailor it yet.
evidence: Repository discovery found no application UI source or pre-existing UI skill.
The new skill exists at the routed path and AGENTS/AI-BOOTSTRAP enforce it before UI code.
entries.rationale: Agents need enforceable production UI procedure while keeping absent application design facts explicit.
consequences: All UI/UX-related work must use the skill, perform reuse discovery, and create a UX Decision Summary before code.
Re-run the UI audit and populate source-backed design/component memory when application sources arrive.
risks: The procedural skill must not be mistaken for evidence of an application design system or component library.

entries.title: Create UI/UX Production skill
entries.summary: Created, routed, audited, and validated the mandatory project-local UI/UX Production skill with progressive references and Project Memory write-back.
evidence: Skill validation passed; all required files and 15 contract fields exist; all 16 rule groups and 40 requested anti-patterns were found; routing and Markdown links passed; memory lint had zero blockers/warnings before task-summary insertion.
entries.rationale: A completed summary preserves the new mandatory UI procedure, validation boundary, and remaining application-source gap without chat dependence.
consequences: All UI/UX work is now gated by Project Memory, the UI skill, reuse discovery, and a UX Decision Summary.
risks: No application source exists, so framework/token/component-specific guidance and runtime UI validation remain pending.

entries.title: Backend Production installed; application backend baseline absent
entries.summary: Backend Production Engineering is mandatory and routed; no application or Base44/backend sources exist to tailor or validate it yet.
evidence: Repository discovery found no application/Base44 backend source or pre-existing backend skill.
The new skill exists at the routed path; AGENTS and AI-BOOTSTRAP enforce it before backend code.
Skill validation passed with 19 contract fields, 14 required references, 70 anti-patterns, valid routing, and valid links.
entries.rationale: Agents need enforceable production backend procedure while absent application/platform guarantees remain explicit.
consequences: All backend/full-stack work must use Project Memory, this skill, capability/reuse discovery, Backend Decision Summary, and pre/post gates.
Re-run the backend audit and populate source-backed capability/domain/data/security/operations memory when application sources arrive.
risks: The procedural skill must not be mistaken for evidence of application architecture, Base44 capabilities, transaction guarantees, permissions, tests, or production readiness.

entries.title: Create Backend Production Engineering skill
entries.summary: Created, routed, audited, and validated the mandatory project-local Backend Production Engineering operating system with progressive references and Project Memory write-back.
evidence: Skill-creator validation logic passed; 19 contract fields, all required references, 70 anti-patterns, routing/gate, Markdown links, metadata, encoding, and unfinished-marker checks passed.
The audit/risk/backlog and Project Memory details were updated without creating application code or fabricated Base44/business/schema facts.
entries.rationale: A completed summary preserves the mandatory backend procedure, validation boundary, and remaining application-source gap without chat dependence.
consequences: All backend/full-stack work is now gated by Project Memory, the backend skill, capability/reuse discovery, Backend Decision Summary, and production validation.
risks: No application source exists, so project-specific architecture/capabilities/contracts and runtime production validation remain pending.

entries.title: Project Engineering Skill System is active
entries.summary: Validated Constitution, Orchestrator, 21-Standard-Skill registry, 12-workflow registry, separate 27-module Custom Skill library, approval gates, routing fixtures, and executable lint are active.
evidence: Skill lint passed for 21 skills with zero blockers and warnings.
Workflow lint passed for 12 workflows with zero blockers and warnings.
Route/link validation passed with 35 anti-patterns; 24 static routing fixtures cover all workflows and four risk levels.
Custom lint validates 27 reusable modules; detector synchronization and 12 custom route fixtures pass.
entries.rationale: Future agents need one durable fact describing the instruction architecture without reading every skill.
consequences: Every task starts with Constitution and Orchestrator routing.
Concrete domain skills remain evidence-gated.
Application production readiness remains unverified.
Custom Skills activate only through evidence-backed task/path-scoped project bindings.
risks: Registries and memory become stale if future instruction changes are not synchronized.

entries.title: Build Project Engineering Skill System
entries.summary: Audited and transformed the instruction-only repository into a validated Constitution-Orchestrator-Memory-Capability-Workflow system with 20 skills and 11 workflows.
evidence: 20 registered skills and 11 workflows exist.
All three system validators passed with zero blockers and warnings.
Project Memory lint passed after indexed write-back.
entries.rationale: Preserve the completed architecture, validation boundary, and application-evidence gap outside chat history.
consequences: Future tasks use Orchestrator and one primary workflow.
Capability and workflow changes require registry and validation synchronization.
risks: No application evidence exists, so application-specific production and domain claims remain unavailable.

entries.title: Reusable production project template baseline is active
entries.summary: The repository has an evidence-gated onboarding, governance, and cross-platform validation baseline while application-specific controls remain conditional.
evidence: Unified local validation covers repository, skills, workflows, routing fixtures, routes/links, and memory.
Static fixtures cover 12 workflows and four risk levels.
CI definition targets Windows and Linux without assuming an application stack.
entries.rationale: Future clones need a durable boundary between reusable template readiness and application production readiness.
consequences: Clones route first-run work through project-onboarding.
Application controls activate only from verified triggers.
External repository settings remain owner-verified.
risks: Hosted CI and repository protections are unverified until pushed and configured.
A clone can become stale if the project profile is not instantiated.

entries.title: Add reusable production template governance and quality baseline
entries.summary: Added evidence-gated project onboarding, repository governance, security/release baselines, static routing fixtures, and cross-platform reusable-layer validation without fabricating application controls.
evidence: 21 skills and 12 workflows are registered.
24 route fixtures cover every workflow and risk level.
Unified local validation passes the reusable layer.
entries.rationale: Preserve the completed reusable baseline and its evidence boundary outside chat history.
consequences: Future clones begin with project-onboarding.
Stack-specific production controls remain conditional.
Repository-host settings require owner verification.
risks: GitHub-hosted checks remain unverified until push and workflow completion.
Application production readiness cannot be inferred from template validation.

entries.title: Two-layer reusable Custom Skill system is active
entries.summary: The repository has 27 reusable Custom Skills, evidence-based stack/domain detection, and manifest-driven project overlays while only GitHub CI is active in template mode.
evidence: 27 reusable modules pass Skill Creator quick validation and custom lint.
Stack detection finds only GitHub CI at HIGH confidence.
Domain detection finds zero qualifying artifacts and zero verified rules.
Twelve custom route fixtures enforce task and path scope.
entries.rationale: Future clones need reusable technology/domain expertise without copying project facts or loading irrelevant skills.
consequences: Standard Skills retain higher authority.
Reusable modules remain inactive until project bindings exist.
Clone onboarding must re-run detection and create scoped overlays.
risks: A clone will block validation if application evidence changes without manifest synchronization.
Hosted CI remains unverified until pushed.

entries.title: Add reusable two-layer Custom Skill system
entries.summary: Added 27 reusable technology/domain skills, evidence detectors, project bindings/overlays, routing integration, validation, reports, and memory without creating application-specific rules.
evidence: Fourteen technology and thirteen domain reusable skill contracts exist.
Twenty-seven skills pass Skill Creator quick validation.
Unified validation composes all four custom validators.
Only the evidence-backed GitHub CI binding is active.
entries.rationale: Preserve the implementation boundary and validated outcome outside chat history.
consequences: Future projects can reuse bases and add only evidence-backed overlays.
Application-specific work remains deferred until real sources exist.
risks: Detection and activation manifests must be regenerated after a clone adds application evidence.
External GitHub enforcement is still owner-verified.
